title: Occult Street Encounters
description: What happens when the crew moves through a city where the occult and the mundane intersect — the things they see, the people they meet, the moments that require a decision.

entries.result: A street vendor selling items that are almost certainly what they appear to be. The one item in the back corner is not.
A person standing still in a moving crowd, staring at a building that looks ordinary from the outside.
A chalk mark on the wall that was not there yesterday and means something specific to anyone who reads these signs.
A pair of investigators talking to the building's super at an address the crew has reason to be interested in.
Someone the crew recognizes doing something in public that they should not be doing in public.
A fight breaking out between two people that is not entirely a fight between two people.
A child offering to sell the crew information about 'what lives in this block' for a coin.
A practitioner the crew knows by reputation but has never met, apparently following someone.
A delivery being made to an address at three in the morning, efficiently, quietly.
A door in an alley that does not match the building it's in — different age, different material, different lock.
A person asking for directions to an address that, as far as the crew knows, doesn't exist.
A newspaper headline about an event the crew was peripherally involved in, framed in a way that is technically accurate and deeply misleading.
An anonymous note left at the crew's current location indicating that someone knows they're there.
A building that the crew has passed before and that looks different — not renovated, just different.
A figure at the end of the block that matches the description of someone currently in custody.
A patrol behaving as though they're looking for something specific rather than patrolling.
A group of people gathered around something on the ground. By the time the crew reaches the spot, there's nothing there.
A shop the crew uses as a resource has a 'closed' sign. The lights inside are on.
Someone passing the crew very quickly who leaves behind a smell — not perfume, not food, something else — that triggers recognition in one crew member.
A conversation overhead in a café that seems designed to be heard. By someone. The crew may be the intended audience or they may not.
A vehicle parked outside a location the crew is watching with no apparent occupant and plates that belong to a city department that doesn't handle this district.
A stray animal following one crew member with unusual persistence.
A street performer whose act is technically impressive and whose eyes aren't tracking the crowd.
A closed building with an open window on an upper floor.
A shadow on the wall that doesn't match anything visible.
A passerby who greets one crew member by name and keeps walking. No one recognizes them.
A locked gate that has been opened — recently, carefully, without damage to the lock.
An advertisement on a wall for a service that is clearly not the service being advertised.
A fresh set of footprints in dust or mud going into a place that the crew would have expected to be empty.
A figure watching the crew from across the street who is, on second look, not there.
A contact who was supposed to be in another part of the city.
The sound of a language the crew doesn't speak coming from a building that was reportedly vacant.
A child's drawing chalked on the pavement that uses symbols the crew recognizes from professional contexts.
An ambulance parked with its lights off outside an address the crew has interest in.
Someone exiting a building quickly and without luggage who immediately flags down transport and gives a distant address.
A smell coming from a drainpipe that indicates something below the street is different than it was.
A wanted notice posted near the crew's current route with a description that is plausibly one of them.
A bookshop with a specific title displayed that the crew knows to be code in certain circles.
A second vehicle following the first vehicle that is following the crew.
A person they are supposed to meet who is early, which has never happened before.
